subject: Sports
title: Carl Lewis: Track Royalty
description: A 60-second lesson on Carl Lewis: Track Royalty.

hook.text: Nine Olympic golds across four Games. Carl Lewis was track and field's most consistent champion.

buildup.text: At Los Angeles 1984, Lewis matched Jesse Owens by winning the 100m, 200m, long jump, and 4x100m relay.

discovery.text: He won the long jump at four consecutive Olympics—1984, 1988, 1992, and 1996. Nobody else has done that.

twist.text: His 1988 100m 'silver' became gold after Ben Johnson's disqualification for doping.

climax.text: Lewis competed at the top level for 17 years, defying the usual career arc of sprinters.

punchline.text: Owens dominated one Games. Lewis dominated an entire era.

quiz.question: How many consecutive Olympics did Carl Lewis win the long jump?
